summary: To continue ensuring Azure remains the best possible experience with the highest standards of safety and reliability,we are retiring the current architecture of AKS enabled by Azure Arc on Windows Server 2019 in March 2026, and on Windows Server 2022 in 3 years, on March 27, 2028. This guide helps you migrate your AKS enabled by Azure Arc workloads from Windows Server 2019 to Windows Server 2022. classification_reason: Contains specific retirement dates (March 2026, March 27, 2028) and a prescriptive migration path for AKS Arc workloads; these timelines and steps are expert, product-specific guidance for upgrade decisions.

title: Use the autoscaler profile to configure cluster autoscaling in AKS on Windows Server - AKS enabled by Azure Arc
display_title: Configure AKS Arc autoscaler profile parameters and defaults
description: Learn how to use the autoscaler profile to configure Cluster autoscaler in Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) on Windows Server.
summary: Applies to: AKS on Windows Server You can use the parameters in the autoscaler profile object to define scale events in AKS on Windows Server. The cluster autoscaler profile affects all node pools that use the cluster autoscaler; you can't set an autoscaler profile per node pool. This article explains how the autoscaler works, describes default autoscaler profile values, and describes how to configure and use a profile definition.
classification_reason: Explicitly states it describes autoscaler profile parameters, default values, and how to configure them; these parameter names, defaults, and allowed ranges are product-specific configuration knowledge.

title: Use PowerShell for cluster autoscaling in AKS on Windows Server - AKS enabled by Azure Arc
description: Learn how to use PowerShell for cluster autoscaling in AKS on Windows Server.
summary: Applies to: AKS on Windows Server You can use PowerShell to enable the autoscaler and to manage automatic scaling of node pools in your target clusters in AKS on Windows Server. You can also use PowerShell to configure and manage cluster autoscaling.
classification_reason: PowerShell-based autoscaling how-to; summary does not explicitly mention autoscaler profile parameters or default values, so it appears more like a procedural tutorial.
